name: Grok 4.5
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (size undisclosed by SpaceXAI); internally referred to in press coverage as a 'V9' generation, a full architecture shift from the V8-series behind Grok 4.3, retrained heavily on real Cursor developer-session data for coding/agentic work
note: SpaceXAI's newest flagship (announced July 8, 2026, public July 9), the first model shipped since the SpaceXAI rebrand and since acquiring Cursor; positioned by Elon Musk as 'Opus-class... but faster, more token-efficient and lower cost' and 'roughly comparable to Opus 4.7, but much faster.' Powers the Grok Build CLI, is available in Cursor on all plans, and via the SpaceXAI console; not yet available in the EU at launch (targeted mid-July 2026). Ranks 4th on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (score 54) behind Fable 5, GPT-5.5, and Opus 4.8, but at roughly 60%+ lower price than Opus 4.8/GPT-5.5, with markedly lower average output-token usage per task (token efficiency claim).

results.notes: SWE-Bench Pro resolve rate, xAI/SpaceXAI's own reported number at launch, 2026-07-08 (below Opus 4.8's 69.2%, above Opus 4.7's 64.3% and GPT-5.5 xhigh's 58.6%); xAI emphasized resolving tasks with ~4.2x fewer output tokens than Opus 4.8.
Terminal-Bench 2.1 (shell-driven agentic tasks), evaluated at launch 2026-07-08; near GPT-5.5's 83.4% and just behind Fable 5's 84.3%.

GPQA Diamond 93.1% (relaying Artificial Analysis's independent evaluation, not an xAI-published number). Cross-corroborated by doit.software/blog/grok-statistics ('Grok 4.5 scores 93% on GPQA Diamond'). Artificial Analysis's own Grok 4.5 model page (directly fetched) confirms 'GPQA Diamond (scientific reasoning)' is one of the individual evaluations feeding its Intelligence Index for this model, supporting that AA does have a real score here even though AA's dynamic leaderboard page did not render the specific row in my fetch. One source (awesomeagents.ai) claimed 'GPQA Diamond... hasn't been published yet,' but this is outweighed by two independent numeric citations plus AA's own page confirming the evaluation was run.

MMMU-Pro 80.4% (Artificial Analysis independent evaluation). Cross-corroborated by doit.software/blog/grok-statistics ('MMMU-Pro: 80%'). AA's own Grok 4.5 model page (directly fetched) lists MMMU-Pro among its individual Intelligence Index evaluations for this model.

AA-HLE (Humanity's Last Exam) 40.3%, from Artificial Analysis's independent evaluation. This specific number is single-sourced (only benchlm.ai reported the exact figure; other aggregators mentioned HLE is part of the Intelligence Index breakdown but did not state the number), so confidence is lower than the other additions here — flagging this explicitly rather than treating it as fully corroborated.

Directly fetched Artificial Analysis's own GDPval-AA v2 leaderboard, which explicitly lists 'Grok 4.5 (high)' at Elo 1539 (rank 12 of 160 models, released July 2026). Two third-party aggregators (felloai.com, awesomeagents.ai) separately cite a slightly different 1543, and benchlm.ai cites 1539 — I used AA's own directly-fetched primary leaderboard figure (1539) over the third-party recaps.
Vals AI independent eval of Grok 4.5, ranked 7/127 models on the platform's LiveCodeBench tracker (fetched July 2026).
Vals AI independent MMLU Pro score, ranked 8/120.

Vals AI independent MMMU score, ranked 72/82 -- a comparatively weak result for Grok 4.5 relative to its other scores; included per this pass's brief to surface less-favorable numbers, not just strong ones.

Vals AI's 'SWE-bench' benchmark; confirmed via vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench (page titled 'SWE-bench Verified') that this is the Verified split, matching this corpus's convention of filing SWE-bench Verified under the base 'swe-bench' slug. Ranked 4/69. Distinct from -- and notably higher than -- the SWE-Bench Pro figure (64.7%) already in this record; Verified and Pro are different task pools of different difficulty, not the same benchmark.

Chatbot Arena (LMArena, rebranded 'Arena' as of Jan 2026) Text leaderboard Elo for Grok 4.5: 1464 (±9), rank #35/many, confirmed via direct fetch of arena.ai/leaderboard/text on 2026-07-15. A third-party aggregator (benchlm.ai) separately cites a slightly different 'Text Overall' figure of 1469 for the same model; I used the primary leaderboard's own directly-fetched number (1464) rather than the aggregator recap, consistent with this corpus's established practice of preferring primary leaderboards over aggregator citations. Not previously tracked for this model (arena slug absent from grok-4-5.json).

Artificial Analysis's launch article for Grok 4.5 states a 33% τ³-Banking score. [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 7/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "Grok 4.5" (xAI). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting.

verificationNotes:
Release date July 8, 2026 (announcement) / July 9 (public availability) corroborated by TechCrunch, Axios, and Artificial Analysis. Pricing $2/$6 per Mtok (75% cache discount to $0.50) corroborated by TechCrunch, Axios, and Artificial Analysis's model page — matches the task's expected price point exactly. Context window of 500K tokens (down from Grok 4.3's 1M) and text+vision-only modalities (no video, unlike 4.3) come from a single source (Artificial Analysis's model page) and could not be independently cross-checked against a second source; flagged as lower-confidence. A widely-repeated '1.5T parameters, 3x larger than predecessor' figure appears in several 'leak'-labeled outlets (CometAPI, DigitalApplied) and one AA-derived summary, but SpaceXAI has not disclosed parameters and Artificial Analysis's own model page explicitly states parameters are undisclosed — I treated the 1.5T figure as an unconfirmed rumor and left paramsB null rather than report it as fact.

COVERAGE-COMPLETENESS PASS (2026-07-14): re-fetched the primary launch blog (x.ai/news/grok-4-5) in full — confirms xAI published exactly four coding/agentic benchmark charts at launch (DeepSWE 1.0, DeepSWE 1.1, SWE Marathon, Terminal-Bench 2.1, SWE-Bench Pro) plus one token-efficiency chart; independently corroborated by apidog.com's benchmark-by-benchmark breakdown ('xAI launched Grok 4.5 ... with four coding benchmarks and one efficiency chart'). Of those, only Terminal-Bench 2.1 and SWE-Bench Pro map to this corpus's taxonomy (both already recorded); DeepSWE 1.0/1.1 and SWE Marathon have no corresponding taxonomy slug and were not forced into one. Also checked Artificial Analysis's own published launch article (artificialanalysis.ai/articles/grok-4-5-brings-spacexai-to-the-the-intelligence-frontier), which additionally states Grok 4.5 scores 33% on τ³-Banking (a newer tau-bench variant with no matching taxonomy slug — our taxonomy only has tau-bench and tau2-bench-telecom, both different evals — so left unmapped) and an alternate GDPval-AA Elo of 1543 (vs. the 1539 already on file from AA's leaderboard page; left the existing verified row untouched rather than second-guessing prior verification). No academic/knowledge benchmarks (MMLU, MMLU-Pro, AIME, MATH, GSM8K, IFEval, BFCL, Arena) have been published by xAI or any independent tracker for this model as of 2026-07-14, six days post-launch — genuine gap, not an omission on my part.

GAP-RESOLUTION PASS (2026-07-16): (1) MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval — re-checked all three required source tiers and found no published score for any of the four on Grok 4.5. Official: x.ai/news/grok-4-5 (per apidog.com's benchmark-by-benchmark breakdown, corroborating the prior direct fetch) published only DeepSWE 1.0/1.1, SWE Marathon, Terminal-Bench 2.1, SWE-Bench Pro, and a token-efficiency chart — no knowledge/math/legacy-coding benchmarks. Independent: Vals.ai's Grok 4.5 model page (directly fetched 2026-07-16) tracks MMLU Pro, GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench, MMMU, SWE-bench (Verified), and Terminal-Bench 2.1 for this model but has no MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval row; Artificial Analysis's model page and Intelligence Index breakdown likewise omit all four; BenchLM.ai's full ~20-metric evaluation table explicitly states it "does not display MMLU, AIME, MATH-500, or HumanEval scores for Grok 4.5." Epoch AI's benchmarking hub URL returned 404 (no dedicated Grok 4.5 page found). Sibling cross-reference: Grok 4 (91.7% AIME 2025 no-tools) and Grok 4 Heavy (100% AIME 2025) carry AIME scores in this corpus, but these are prior-generation models on a different architecture ('V8-series' vs. 4.5's 'V9') and their scores cannot be attributed to Grok 4.5 — no 4.5-specific figure exists anywhere I could find. All four are treated as a genuine, dated (2026-07-16) absence: consistent with the pattern already documented in this file's 2026-07-14 note and with the broader industry shift away from saturated legacy benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval) toward their harder successors (MMLU-Pro, GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified), all four of which are already recorded for this model. (2) swe-bench=86.6 comparability check — re-verified directly via vals.ai/benchmarks/swebench (fetched 2026-07-16): page title is literally "SWE-bench Verified," matching this corpus's own benchmarks.json catalog, where the 'swe-bench' slug's display name is "SWE-bench Verified" (distinct from the separate 'swe-bench-pro' slug used for the 64.7% entry). The existing entry's notes already state this explicitly and correctly; no relabeling or drop was needed — the flagged comparability concern does not apply to the current file content and appears to have been resolved in an earlier verification round.

SECOND-PASS DEEP GAP RESEARCH (2026-07-17): Re-attacked the four missing legacy benchmarks (MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval) using six new source types explicitly not used in the prior passes (which had covered x.ai's own launch page, Artificial Analysis, Vals.ai, LMArena/Arena, and BenchLM). Result: still no addition possible for any of the four -- every new source either omits Grok 4.5 entirely or, where it lists benchmark values for "Grok," those values belong to a different, older model in the same family (Grok 4, Grok 4.20, Grok-1/1.5/2), not Grok 4.5. Detail per source type checked:

(1) Stanford HELM -- fetched crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities/latest/ and cross-checked via search. HELM Capabilities' evaluated model roster is limited to OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/etc. checkpoints from its own curated run list; no xAI/Grok model of any version appears on HELM Capabilities, Classic, or Lite leaderboards. HELM does not test proprietary xAI models via API at all, so there is no HELM-run MMLU score to recover for this or any Grok generation -- genuine structural absence, not a Grok-4.5-specific gap.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar -- ran multiple targeted searches ("Grok 4.5" MMLU site:arxiv.org, MATH-500, AIME 2025, HumanEval) and attempted the Semantic Scholar Graph API directly (rate-limited both attempts, 429). Web-search-indexed arXiv results turned up only the GLM-4.5 technical report (arxiv.org/pdf/2508.06471), which cites "Grok 4" (not 4.5) at MMLU-Pro 84.6 as a comparison baseline -- a prior-generation model on different architecture, not attributable to 4.5. No arXiv paper anywhere cites Grok 4.5 by name with an MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figure.

(3) GitHub eval-harness / data-mirror repos -- ran `gh search code` across four queries (grok-4.5 + MMLU / humaneval / AIME / MATH-500). Two genuinely useful finds: (a) reyamira/models `data/v2/epoch.json`, a GitHub-committed mirror of Epoch AI's own benchmarking-hub API (source block: `"id": "epoch", "url": "https://epoch.ai/benchmarks", "fetched_at": "2026-07-17T18:18:50Z", "verified": true`). Its full metric catalog (~24 metrics: ARC-AGI, ARC-AGI-2, Epoch Capabilities Index, FrontierMath, FrontierMath Tier 4, APEX Agents, PostTrainBench, SWE-bench Verified, WeirdML, ALE-Bench, BlueprintBench 2, BTF3, Chess Puzzles, CritPT, CursorBench, DeepResearchBench, DeepSWE, FrontierCode, FrontierSWE, GPQA Diamond, GBAEval, OTIS Mock AIME 2024-2025, OSWorld 2, ProofBench, SciCode, SimpleBench, SimpleQA Verified, Surface Evolver Bench, Vending-Bench 2) contains **zero** entries for plain MMLU, MATH(-500), real/competition AIME, or HumanEval -- confirming Epoch AI has simply retired these four from its current tracking suite across ALL models, not just Grok 4.5 (this explains and corroborates the 404 the first pass hit on Epoch's site). The grok-4.5 model record itself lists: gpqa_diamond 0.9343, otis_mock_aime_2024_2025 0.9778, scicode 0.5405, simplebench 0.70, simpleqa_verified 0.535, epoch_capabilities_index 153.17, chess_puzzles 0.36, proofbench 0.30, blueprint_bench_2 0.2733, surface_evolver_bench 0.744 -- none mappable to this corpus's mmlu/math/aime/humaneval slugs. Note on OTIS Mock AIME specifically: this corpus's 'aime' slug is explicitly defined as "American Invitational Mathematics Examination, pass@1" (the real competition), whereas OTIS Mock AIME is Epoch's own distinct mock-problem set in AIME style -- a different eval, consistent with this corpus's established practice (see existing τ³-Banking and GDPval-AA notes) of not conflating adjacent-but-non-identical benchmarks under one slug. Left unmapped rather than forced. (b) ariobarin/which-llm `skills/which-llm/artifacts/models_enriched.csv`, a GitHub-committed scrape of Artificial Analysis's own model table, has an explicit row for "Grok 4.5 (high)" with named columns for mmlu_pro, math_500, aime, aime25, and humaneval -- all four are blank/null in the row, while adjacent columns (gpqa, hle, mmmu_pro, tau2) are populated. This is a second independent data mirror corroborating that AA itself has not run/published these four evals for Grok 4.5, matching what the first pass found by directly fetching AA's page.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators -- OpenRouter's Grok 4.5 page (openrouter.ai/x-ai/grok-4.5) has a "Benchmarks" section header but no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval values rendered for this model. lmmarketcap.com/benchmarks/math_500 lists Grok 4 (95%), Grok 3 (85%), and Grok 2 (76.1%) on its MATH-500 leaderboard but has no Grok 4.5 row at all. lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks lists Grok 4, Grok 4 Fast, and Grok 4.1 Fast with no scores shown for the four target benchmarks, and no Grok 4.5 entry. RankedAGI (rankedagi.com) shows Grok 4.5 with proprietary composite categories (Overall 69.6, Coding 83.3, Reasoning 71.8, Agentic 82.0, "Math" 65.2) but its methodology page (linked, not directly fetchable) was not confirmed to decompose "Math" into AIME/MATH-500 specifically, and the category is explicitly a blend, not a discrete benchmark score -- excluded as not citable as a specific MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figure. LiveCodeBench's own leaderboard and LiveBench.ai were checked via search; neither surfaced a Grok-4.5-specific HumanEval-adjacent or knowledge score beyond what's already recorded (LiveCodeBench itself is already in this record).

(5) Non-English sources -- Chinese-language and Japanese-language searches ("Grok 4.5 格罗克 MMLU 数学", "Grok 4.5 グロック ベンチマーク MMLU") surfaced only generic marketing descriptions (lobehub locale files, translated verbatim across zh-CN/zh-TW/de/nl/pt-BR/pl/es/ko/tr, e.g. "SpaceXAI's flagship model ... coding ability on par with Claude Opus" -- no numbers) plus historical context for older Grok generations (Grok-1 ~73% MMLU, Grok-1.5 81.3%, Grok-2 87.5%, "Grok 4.20 scores 91.2% MMLU" -- a distinct, earlier model, not 4.5). No region-specific blog or forum post was found independently benchmarking Grok 4.5 itself on any of the four target evals.

(6) Sibling models / same-family comparison tables -- janwilmake/state-of-llm-apis GitHub repo's `models/xai.md` (a maintained xAI model catalog) explicitly lists, under its separate "### Grok 4 (Released 2025-07-09)" section, "GPQA Diamond: 87.7% | MMLU: 86.6% | LiveCodeBench: 81.9%" -- confirmed by fetching the full file that this line sits under the older Grok 4 entry, not the Grok 4.5 entry immediately above it in the same document (whose own metrics table lists only API names, context window, and pricing -- zero benchmark scores). This is the clearest same-repo, same-family control case: the file's author had MMLU data for the sibling/predecessor model and chose not to (or could not) populate it for Grok 4.5, reinforcing that the omission is a genuine data gap rather than an oversight on this corpus's part. GLM-4.5's technical report (source type 2 above) shows the same pattern: Grok 4 has a citable score, Grok 4.5 does not. A separate lead -- openstory-so/openstory `src/lib/ai/models.config.ts`, whose GitHub search snippet showed "MMLU 94.2" near a grok-4.5 reference -- was run down by fetching the full file; that MMLU figure belongs to an unrelated (mislabeled "MIT license") catalog entry elsewhere in the file, not to the Grok 4.5 record (which has no benchmark fields at all, only id/name/provider/license/qualityRank/contextWindow). Discarded as a false match.

Conclusion: six of six new source types were checked (exceeding the 3-type minimum), all independently converging on the same result. As of 2026-07-17, nine days post-launch, no vendor, independent lab, academic paper, GitHub-mirrored eval log, aggregator, non-English source, or sibling-model comparison table has published an MMLU, MATH(-500), real-AIME, or HumanEval score for Grok 4.5 specifically. This is consistent with -- and further reinforces -- the 2026-07-14 and 2026-07-16 passes' conclusion that xAI/SpaceXAI, Epoch AI, and Artificial Analysis have all collectively moved on from these four saturated/legacy benchmarks for this model generation. No scores added; all four gaps remain open, now with substantially broadened source coverage documented.
